message:
Pre-allocated scratch ABI: zero per-call alloc overhead

Adopts the gomode/edgebox pattern — pre-allocate scratch regions once
at WASM init, reuse every call. No more resetHeap()+alloc() per operation.

- 8MB I/O scratch buffer: input data written directly (no alloc call)
- 20-byte SHA output slot: hash results read as zero-copy view
- 8-byte scalar slot: consumed counts via DataView (no alloc)
- _resetDynamic() replaces resetHeap(): frees dynamic allocs, preserves scratch
- heapSave/heapRestore checkpoint separates scratch from dynamic region
- Oversized inputs (>8MB) fall back to dynamic alloc transparently
- Applied to both WasmEngine and WasmEngineCore
